dialogos.texto_en: (After finishing sightseeing at Shurijo Castle and\n having dinner at an Okinawan cuisine restaurant, we\n returned to the hotel, feeling fulfilled by the day.)
(By the time we returned, the sun had already set%p-2;――%p;)
(And there was a magnificent night view\n of the Okinawan city beneath our gazes.)
Ohh, the city lights look like the sea!
Yes, it's beautiful.\nThe hotel recommendation was worth it.
(After hearing Itsuki's conversation,\n I was convinced. They were all waiting for me\n to come up with an answer.)
By the way, I heard that the night view\nfrom the observation deck at Shurijo Castle\nis also impressive.
Oh, you mean the Iri-no-Azana lookout?\nThe view was certainly amazing.
(If I'm going to convey my determination,\n I should do it soon. I'm prepared.\n Everyone is here now, so%p-2;――%p;)
Hey, Futaro-kun!\nCome over here and take a look too!
Sorry, I'll see the night view later.\nThere's something I want to tell you first.
...I see. It seems like an important conversation.
Yeah.
Finally.
Okay.
I'm ready. Please go ahead, Uesugi-san.
(The atmosphere has changed.\n Now, how do I convey what I want to say...)
When you make such a serious face,\nit's a bit hard to say...
Just because of that,\nit doesn't mean we can't\nhave a cheerful conversation, right?
Well, that's true.
First off, I think I understand the feelings\nthat led you to propose this trip.
I'm not sure if I can say it well...
Please tell us in your own words, Uesugi-san.\nThat's good enough.
I'm sorry.\nIt's frustrating to have practically no experience\nwith this kind of thing.
First off, I think I understand the feelings\nthat led you to propose this trip.
It seems like you already know, so I won't mince my words.
That's right. We don't need any more complications.
It's finally your turn, Futaro.
...Yeah.
First off, I think I understand the feelings\nthat led you to propose this trip.
At first, I was surprised.\nAfter all, I had been thinking the same thing.
It's only been two days since we came to Okinawa,\nbut your...strong feelings, if I may call it that,\nhave made me realize it once again.
Leaving you guys alone and going to Tokyo by myself\nwould be no different than running away.
For the sake of our future,\nI want to settle this relationship between us.
I really love you five.\nThat hasn't changed even now.
However, as a man,\nI think I should come up with just one answer.
I know it's selfish of me to say that\nafter not choosing anyone, but%p-2;――
I will give you an answer during this trip.\nAs of right now, that's how I truly feel.
...We've been waiting for you to say that.
You made us wait too long.
Yes. Finally.
I'm so sorry for making you wait.
It seems like Uesugi-san has made up his mind.\nPlanning this trip was a good idea.
...During the trip, huh?
Itsuki?
Uesugi-kun, you're selfish.
Dragging this on, then suddenly choosing one person,\ncan you really do that?
And, after running away once!
W-well...
(At that time, I believed that choosing nobody\n was the best decision.)
Because of the decision you made at that time,\neveryone suffered, and was sad...
(But Itsuki is right. In the end,\n it was all because of my indecisiveness.)
Can you really say that you won't repeat the same mistake?
(That's why I can't run away this time.)
Choosing someone means not choosing everyone else.
Are you saying you have the resolve to do that now?
Itsuki-chan, calm down a bit.
You're going a bit too far...
We decided together\nthat we'll have Fu-kun give us an answer, right?
If Futaro makes the decision for us,\nI'll be satisfied with that alone.
That's not the point.\nThis is Uesugi-kun's own problem, not ours.
When you choose something,\nyou're also not choosing something else. That's true.
Even so...\nthere will come a day\nwhen we have to make up our minds.
It's too late for me to ask you to believe me now, but%p-2;――
This time, I'll definitely give you an answer.
...There really is a limit to being selfish.
However, I'm happy that you said\nyou'll come up with the answer yourself,\nwhen you thought about us.
So, this is my chance to test that resolve of yours.
Sure. If that will satisfy you.
Then, you'll have to go through a trial.
A trial...?
Yes. I'll tell you the details tomorrow.
Hold on. Ichika, do you know anything about this?
No. First time for me too.
Itsuki, explain.
(Do the others not know either?\n What is she planning to do?)
It's getting late today.\nLet's call it a day.
Eh, hey?!
Alright then, Uesugi-kun.\nSee you tomorrow.
